sections.name: Establish input data policy
sections.description: Establish and communicate AI input data policies covering how customer data is used for model training, inference processing, data retention periods, and customer data rights

sections.name: Establish output data policy
sections.description: Establish AI output ownership, usage, opt-out and deletion policies to customers and communicate these policies

sections.name: Limit AI agent data collection
sections.description: Implement safeguards to limit AI agent data access to task-relevant information based on user roles and context

sections.name: Protect IP & trade secrets
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent AI systems from leaking company intellectual property or confidential information

sections.name: Prevent cross-customer data exposure
sections.description: Implement safeguards to prevent cross-customer data exposure when combining customer data from multiple sources

sections.name: Prevent PII leakage
sections.description: Establish safeguards to prevent personal data leakage through AI outputs and logs

sections.name: Prevent IP violations
sections.description: Implement safeguards and technical controls to prevent AI outputs from violating copyrights, trademarks, or other third-party intellectual property rights

sections.name: Third-party testing of adversarial robustness
sections.description: Implement adversarial testing program to validate system resilience against adversarial inputs and prompt injection attempts in line with adversarial threat taxonomy

sections.name: Detect adversarial input
sections.description: Implement monitoring capabilities to detect and respond to adversarial inputs and prompt injection attempts

sections.name: Manage public release of technical details
sections.description: Implement controls to prevent over-disclosure of technical information about AI systems and organizational details that could enable adversarial targeting

sections.name: Prevent AI endpoint scraping
sections.description: Implement safeguards to prevent probing or scraping of external AI endpoints

sections.name: Implement real-time input filtering
sections.description: Implement real-time input filtering using automated moderation tools

sections.name: Prevent unauthorized AI agent actions
sections.description: Implement safeguards to prevent AI agents from performing actions beyond intended scope and authorized privileges

sections.name: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems
sections.description: Establish and maintain user access controls and admin privileges for AI systems in line with policy

sections.name: Protect model deployment environment
sections.description: Implement security measures for AI model deployment environments including encryption, access controls and authorization

sections.name: Limit output over-exposure
sections.description: Implement output limitations and obfuscation techniques to safeguard against information leakage

sections.name: Define AI risk taxonomy
sections.description: Establish a risk taxonomy that categorizes risks within harmful, out-of-scope, and hallucinated outputs, tool calls, and other risks based on application-specific usage

sections.name: Conduct pre-deployment testing
sections.description: Conduct internal testing of AI systems prior to deployment across risk categories for system changes requiring formal review or approval

sections.name: Prevent harmful outputs
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent harmful outputs including distressed outputs, angry responses, high-risk advice, offensive content, bias, and deception

sections.name: Prevent out-of-scope outputs
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent out-of-scope outputs (e.g. political discussion, healthcare advice)

sections.name: Prevent customer-defined high risk outputs
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent additional high risk outputs as defined in risk taxonomy

sections.name: Prevent output vulnerabilities
sections.description: Implement safeguards to prevent security vulnerabilities in outputs from impacting users

sections.name: Flag high risk outputs
sections.description: Implement an alerting system that flags high-risk outputs for human review

sections.name: Monitor AI risk categories
sections.description: Implement monitoring of AI systems across risk categories

sections.name: Enable real-time feedback and intervention
sections.description: Implement mechanisms to enable real-time user feedback collection and intervention mechanisms

sections.name: Third-party testing for harmful outputs
sections.description: Appoint expert third parties to evaluate system robustness to harmful outputs including distressed outputs, angry responses, high-risk advice, offensive content, bias, and deception at least every 3 months

sections.name: Third-party testing for out-of-scope outputs
sections.description: Appoint expert third parties to evaluate system robustness to out-of-scope outputs at least every 3 months (e.g. political discussion, healthcare advice)

sections.name: Third-party testing for customer-defined risk
sections.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate system robustness to additional high-risk outputs as defined in risk taxonomy at least every 3 months

sections.name: Prevent hallucinated outputs
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent hallucinated outputs

sections.name: Third-party testing for hallucinations
sections.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate hallucinated outputs at least every 3 months

sections.name: Restrict unsafe tool calls
sections.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent tool calls in AI systems from executing unauthorized actions, accessing restricted information, or making decisions beyond their intended scope

sections.name: Third-party testing of tool calls
sections.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate tool calls in AI systems, including executing unauthorized actions, accessing restricted information, or making decisions beyond their intended scope at least every 3 months

sections.name: AI failure plan for security breaches
sections.description: Document AI failure plan for AI privacy and security breaches assigning accountable owners and establishing notification and remediation with third-party support as needed (e.g. legal, PR, insurers)

sections.name: AI failure plan for harmful outputs
sections.description: Document AI failure plan for harmful AI outputs that cause significant customer harm assigning accountable owners and establishing remediation with third-party support as needed (e.g. legal, PR, insurers)

sections.name: AI failure plan for hallucinations
sections.description: Document AI failure plan for hallucinated AI outputs that cause substantial customer financial loss assigning accountable owners and establishing remediation with third-party support as needed (e.g. legal, PR, insurers)

sections.name: Assign accountability
sections.description: Document which AI system changes across the development & deployment lifecycle require formal review or approval, assign a lead accountable for each, and document their approval with supporting evidence

sections.name: Assess cloud vs on-prem processing
sections.description: Establish criteria for selecting cloud provider, and circumstances for on-premises processing considering data sensitivity, regulatory requirements, security controls, and operational needs

sections.name: Conduct vendor due diligence
sections.description: Establish AI vendor due diligence processes for foundation and upstream model providers covering data handling, PII controls, security and compliance

sections.name: [Retired] Document system change approvals
sections.description: Merged with E004 - see changelog (Q1 2026 update)

sections.name: Review internal processes
sections.description: Establish regular internal reviews of key processes and document review records and approvals

sections.name: Monitor third-party access
sections.description: Implement systems to monitor third party access

sections.name: Establish AI acceptable use policy
sections.description: Establish and implement an AI acceptable use policy

sections.name: Record processing locations
sections.description: Document AI data processing locations

sections.name: Document regulatory compliance
sections.description: Document applicable AI laws and standards, required data protections, and strategies for compliance

sections.name: Implement quality management system
sections.description: Establish a quality management system for AI systems proportionate to the size of the organization

sections.name: Share transparency reports
sections.description: Merged with E017 - see changelog (Q1 2026 update)

sections.name: Log model activity
sections.description: Maintain logs of AI system processes, actions, and model outputs where permitted to support incident investigation, auditing, and explanation of AI system behavior

sections.name: Implement AI disclosure mechanisms
sections.description: Implement clear disclosure mechanisms to inform users when they are interacting with AI systems rather than humans

sections.name: Document system transparency policy
sections.description: Establish a system transparency policy and maintain a repository of model cards, datasheets, and interpretability reports for major systems

sections.name: Prevent AI cyber misuse
sections.description: Implement or document guardrails to prevent AI-enabled misuse for cyber attacks and exploitation

sections.name: Prevent catastrophic misuse
sections.description: Implement or document guardrails to prevent AI-enabled catastrophic system misuse (chemical / bio / radio / nuclear)
